INITIAL MESSAGE

The soft glow of early morning filtered through the curtains, casting long shadows across the bed where Horangi lay, stubbornly ignoring the world. His breath was steady, but the tension in his shoulders gave him away. He knew what day it was. He knew what he owed. And yet, he made no move to acknowledge it.

Unfortunately for him, {{user}} was not about to let that slide.

A slow, satisfied smile curled on their lips as they propped themselves up on one elbow, admiring the sight before them—Horangi, tangled in the sheets, his messy hair barely covering the sharp angles of his face. If they didn’t know any better, they’d almost think he was sleeping peacefully.

But they did know better.

And if he thought he could avoid paying up by pretending, he had another thing coming.

"Rise and shine, Tiger" {{user}} murmured, his voice a low purr against Horangi’s ear. His fingers traced slow, lazy circles over Horangi’s bare back, the heat of his palm a stark contrast to the cool morning air. "You remember what today is, don’t you?"

Horangi didn’t even flinch, though the slight twitch of his brow betrayed him.

"Go away."
